返回本批次

显示 HN：Atomic – 自托管、语义连接的个人知识库

Show HN: Atomic – Self-hosted, semantically-connected personal knowledge base

github.com 作者 kenforthewin 分数 119 评论 17 2026/03/22 03:31
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 一个个人知识库，将 Markdown 笔记转化为语义连接、由 AI 增强的知识图谱。Atomic 将知识以原子形式存储——Markdown 笔记会被自动分块、嵌入、打标签，并通过语义相似性进行链接。你的原子可以被综合成维基文章，在空间画布上探索，并通过代理式聊天界面进行查询。
  2. 功能
  3. Atoms——支持分层标签、来源 URL 和自动分块的 Markdown 笔记 语义搜索——使用 sqlite-vec 在你的知识库上进行向量搜索 Canvas——力导向空间可视化，语义相似度决定布局 Wiki 综合——由 LLM 基于你的笔记生成带内联引用的文章 聊天——在对话过程中搜索你的知识库的智能体 RAG 界面 自动标签——由 LLM 驱动的标签提取，并组织为分层类别 多 AI 提供商——可使用 OpenRouter（云端）或 Ollama（本地）进行嵌入和 LLM RSS 订阅源——订阅源并自动同步新文章为 atoms 浏览器扩展——将网页内容直接捕获到 Atomic MCP 服务器——将你的知识库暴露给 Claude 和其他 AI 工具 多数据库——多个知识库，共享一个注册表 iOS 应用——用于在移动端读取和编写 atoms 的原生 SwiftUI 客户端
  4. 快速开始 Atomic 可作为桌面应用（Tauri）、无头服务器（Docker/Fly.io），或两者同时运行。 桌面应用 从 GitHub Releases 下载适用于你平台的最新版本（macOS、Linux、Windows）。 首次启动时，设置向导会引导你完成 AI 提供商配置。 使用 Docker Compose 自托管 git clone https://github.com/kenforthewin/atomic.git cd atomic docker compose up -d 这将启动 API 服务器和 Web 前端。 打开 http://localhost 并通过设置向导认领你的实例。 部署到 Fly.io cp fly.toml.example fly.toml fly launch --copy-config --no-deploy fly volumes create atomic_data --region <your-region> --size 1 fly deploy 打开 https://your-app.fly.dev 并认领你的实例。 用于 OAuth/MCP 的公开 URL 会根据 Fly 应用名称自动检测。 独立服务器 cargo run -p atomic-server -- --data-dir ./data serve --port 8080 首次运行时，创建一个 API 令牌： cargo run -p atomic-server -- --data-dir ./data token create --name default AI 提供商设置 Atomic 需要一个 AI 提供商来进行嵌入、标签、Wiki 生成和聊天。
  5. OpenRouter（云端）— 从 openrouter.ai 获取一个 API 密钥。支持为嵌入、标记、Wiki 和聊天分别选择模型。Ollama（本地）— 安装 Ollama 并拉取模型（例如，ollama pull nomic-embed-text）。Atomic 会自动发现可用模型。

Original Summary

  1. A personal knowledge base that turns markdown notes into a semantically-connected, AI-augmented knowledge graph. Atomic stores knowledge as atoms — markdown notes that are automatically chunked, embedded, tagged, and linked by semantic similarity. Your atoms can be synthesized into wiki articles, explored on a spatial canvas, and queried through an agentic chat interface.
  2. Features
  3. Atoms — Markdown notes with hierarchical tagging, source URLs, and automatic chunking Semantic Search — Vector search over your knowledge base using sqlite-vec Canvas — Force-directed spatial visualization where semantic similarity determines layout Wiki Synthesis — LLM-generated articles with inline citations, built from your notes Chat — Agentic RAG interface that searches your knowledge base during conversation Auto-Tagging — LLM-powered tag extraction organized into hierarchical categories Multiple AI Providers — OpenRouter (cloud) or Ollama (local) for embeddings and LLMs RSS Feeds — Subscribe to feeds and automatically sync new articles as atoms Browser Extension — Capture web content directly into Atomic MCP Server — Expose your knowledge base to Claude and other AI tools Multi-Database — Multiple knowledge bases with a shared registry iOS App — Native SwiftUI client for reading and writing atoms on mobile
  4. Getting Started Atomic runs as a desktop app (Tauri), a headless server (Docker/Fly.io), or both. Desktop App Download the latest release for your platform from GitHub Releases (macOS, Linux, Windows). On first launch, the setup wizard walks you through AI provider configuration. Self-Host with Docker Compose git clone https://github.com/kenforthewin/atomic.git cd atomic docker compose up -d This starts the API server and web frontend. Open http://localhost and claim your instance through the setup wizard. Deploy to Fly.io cp fly.toml.example fly.toml fly launch --copy-config --no-deploy fly volumes create atomic_data --region <your-region> --size 1 fly deploy Open https://your-app.fly.dev and claim your instance. The public URL for OAuth/MCP is auto-detected from the Fly app name. Standalone Server cargo run -p atomic-server -- --data-dir ./data serve --port 8080 On first run, create an API token: cargo run -p atomic-server -- --data-dir ./data token create --name default AI Provider Setup Atomic needs an AI provider for embeddings, tagging, wiki generation, and chat.
  5. OpenRouter (cloud) — Get an API key from openrouter.ai. Supports separate model selection for embedding, tagging, wiki, and chat. Ollama (local) — Install Ollama and pull models (e.g., ollama pull nomic-embed-text). Atomic auto-discovers available models.

评论（默认中文）

redm 2026/03/22 07:06
This is pretty cool!

What's holding me back from AI repos and agents isn't running it locally though. Its the lack of granular control. I'm not even sure what I want. I certainly don't want to approve every request, but the idea of large amounts of personal data being accessible, unchecked, to an AI is concerning.

I think perhaps an agent that focuses just on security, that learns about your personal preferences, is what might be needed.

This is pretty cool!

What's holding me back from AI repos and agents isn't running it locally though. Its the lack of granular control. I'm not even sure what I want. I certainly don't want to approve every request, but the idea of large amounts of personal data being accessible, unchecked, to an AI is concerning.

I think perhaps an agent that focuses just on security, that learns about your personal preferences, is what might be needed.

在 HN 中查看
kenforthewin 2026/03/22 07:45
Thanks for taking a look!

Agreed regarding the privacy/security hesitations. Running the models locally with ollama is an option, but of course there's the hardware requirements and limitations of open source models to contend with. ultimately it's a balance between privacy and ease of use, and I'm not sure that there's a good one-size-fits-all for that balance.

Thanks for taking a look!

Agreed regarding the privacy/security hesitations. Running the models locally with ollama is an option, but of course there's the hardware requirements and limitations of open source models to contend with. ultimately it's a balance between privacy and ease of use, and I'm not sure that there's a good one-size-fits-all for that balance.

在 HN 中查看
tedmiston 2026/03/22 13:21
is your idea of granular control (roughly) a group of agents in separate containers writing back to their own designated store each sufficient, or more control than that?
is your idea of granular control (roughly) a group of agents in separate containers writing back to their own designated store each sufficient, or more control than that?
在 HN 中查看
aavci 2026/03/22 12:09
Does anybody mind explaining how the web of articles in the first image helps the writer?
Does anybody mind explaining how the web of articles in the first image helps the writer?
在 HN 中查看
kenforthewin 2026/03/22 13:17
The honest answer is it doesn't help a ton, at least not in its current form. It's fun to look at, and occasionally I'll see some interesting semantic connections between articles - but by far the more useful tools here are the wiki generation, auto-tagging, and chat/MCP features. The graph view definitely needs more love - if anyone has thoughts on how to make it more useful, I'd love to hear them.
The honest answer is it doesn't help a ton, at least not in its current form. It's fun to look at, and occasionally I'll see some interesting semantic connections between articles - but by far the more useful tools here are the wiki generation, auto-tagging, and chat/MCP features. The graph view definitely needs more love - if anyone has thoughts on how to make it more useful, I'd love to hear them.

My first thought is that this would be a great place to find new topics of interest at the "overlap" regions.

Notjoanbaez 2026/03/22 15:47
Nice ! Congratulations.

Not 100% sure what are the ingestions methods available ? Browser extension clipper and RSS are two. I guess I can manually create a node/atom ? Can it scan a local folder for markdown notes ? Or ocr some pdf -> markdown/frontmatter sidecar files -> atomic node ? That would be the dream.

Nice ! Congratulations.

Not 100% sure what are the ingestions methods available ? Browser extension clipper and RSS are two. I guess I can manually create a node/atom ? Can it scan a local folder for markdown notes ? Or ocr some pdf -> markdown/frontmatter sidecar files -> atomic node ? That would be the dream.

jsmo 2026/03/22 17:39
This looks pretty neat! Thanks for sharing ^_^

I saw sqlite-vec for semantic search so I assume notes are stored in sqlite.

- What considerations did you have for the storage layer?

- Also does storage on disk increase linearly as notes/atoms grow?

This looks pretty neat! Thanks for sharing ^_^

andreygrehov 2026/03/22 07:27
Great work, but your macOS build cannot be opened. You need to sign the app through Apple Developer Program.
Great work, but your macOS build cannot be opened. You need to sign the app through Apple Developer Program.
在 HN 中查看
kenforthewin 2026/03/22 07:40
Thanks! The project is still early stages, haven't had a chance to get the app signing set up - right now the easiest way to get started is using the web interface via docker compose.
Thanks! The project is still early stages, haven't had a chance to get the app signing set up - right now the easiest way to get started is using the web interface via docker compose.

actionfromafar 2026/03/22 07:40
System Settings > Privacy & Security, scroll down to the "Security" section, and click Open Anyway
System Settings > Privacy & Security, scroll down to the "Security" section, and click Open Anyway
在 HN 中查看
ukuina 2026/03/22 07:01
Seems like a LogSeq/Roam/Obsidian alternative?
Seems like a LogSeq/Roam/Obsidian alternative?
在 HN 中查看
kenforthewin 2026/03/22 07:26
For sure. The idea here (or at least how I've been using it) is to use Atomic as catchall place to put personal notes, interesting articles, research ideas .. pretty much anything, and Atomic will handle the categorization and knowledge synthesis. For example, I have a knowledge base that uses RSS to sync top Hacker News articles and I'll occasionally generate new wiki-style articles which summarize and synthesize the articles based on top-level categories (AI, hardware, philosophy, you name it).
For sure. The idea here (or at least how I've been using it) is to use Atomic as catchall place to put personal notes, interesting articles, research ideas .. pretty much anything, and Atomic will handle the categorization and knowledge synthesis. For example, I have a knowledge base that uses RSS to sync top Hacker News articles and I'll occasionally generate new wiki-style articles which summarize and synthesize the articles based on top-level categories (AI, hardware, philosophy, you name it).
